question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

graph.answer:
The runtime’s main flow is:

- `Runtime::new()` builds a configured runtime through the builder path in [runtime.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L171).
- `Runtime::spawn()` and `Handle::spawn()` both funnel into scheduler-specific task creation, ending in `Handle::spawn_named()` and then the scheduler handle’s `spawn()` in [runtime.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L239) and [handle.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs#L197).
- `Runtime::block_on()` is the entry point for running a future to completion. It enters the runtime context, then dispatches to the scheduler implementation in [runtime.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L340) and [handle.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs#L342).
- The actual execution loop for the current-thread scheduler lives in `current_thread::Context`: `run_task()` sets up TLS, runs the future under cooperative budgeting, then `park()` / `park_internal()` block on the driver and wake deferred work in [current_thread/mod.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs#L384).
- Runtime shutdown/cleanup is in `Runtime::drop()` and the shutdown helpers in [runtime.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L502).

If you want the shortest reading path, read these in order:

1. [runtime.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L131)  
   Start here for the public API surface: `new`, `spawn`, `block_on`, `enter`, and shutdown.
2. [handle.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs#L39)  
   This shows how `Handle::enter`, `Handle::spawn`, and `Handle::block_on` delegate into the runtime internals.
3. [scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs#L381)  
   This is the “does the work” path for current-thread execution: task polling, parking, driver wakeups, and TLS core handling.
4. [scheduler/multi_thread/worker.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/worker.rs#L572) and [scheduler/multi_thread/mod.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/mod.rs)  
   `Runtime::block_on()` also dispatches here when the multi-thread scheduler is enabled.
5. [context/blocking.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/context/blocking.rs#L59)  
   Useful if your change touches `block_on` behavior or blocking-region semantics.

Tests I’d read next:

1. [runtime/tests/loom_multi_thread.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/tests/loom_multi_thread.rs)  
   Best nearby coverage for scheduler concurrency and multi-thread runtime behavior.
2. [sync/tests/loom_notify.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/sync/tests/loom_notify.rs)  
   Good signal for wake/signal correctness, which is tightly related to park/unpark flow.
3. [sync/tests/loom_atomic_waker.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/sync/tests/loom_atomic_waker.rs)  
   Useful if your change affects waker registration or notification paths.
4. [sync/tests/loom_mpsc.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/sync/tests/loom_mpsc.rs)  
   Relevant for task handoff and wake-driven scheduling interactions.
5. [sync/tests/loom_broadcast.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/sync/tests/loom_broadcast.rs)  
   Another wake-heavy regression surface.
6. [runtime/time_alt/cancellation_queue/tests.rs](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/time_alt/cancellation_queue/tests.rs)  
   More peripheral, but useful if you end up near driver/timer cancellation behavior.

One practical note: I didn’t see a dedicated end-to-end test for the exact `Runtime::block_on` / `Runtime::spawn` path in the surfaced source. The nearest safety net is the Loom coverage around scheduler and waker behavior, so that’s where I’d look first before changing runtime semantics.
